oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_config_merge.json.description: Merging of configuration sources to build the effective settings. The order of precedence is explicit call arguments over configuration-file answers over built-in defaults. Output reports the resolved selection lists and the destination folder name.

feature11_status_report.json.description: The per-entry action report produced while rendering. A fresh destination yields create actions for every produced folder and file. Re-rendering over an existing destination yields an identical action for unchanged files and a conflict action for changed files, followed by a force action when overwriting is forced or a skip action when preserving is requested. Output lists the actions and their targets.

cases.expected_output:
lines=3
create a.txt
create sub/
create sub/b.txt

lines=3
conflict change.txt
force change.txt
identical keep.txt

lines=3
conflict change.txt
skip change.txt
identical keep.txt

feature1_render_engine.json.description: Rendering of a source template tree into a destination. Files whose name carries the template suffix have their contents expanded with the templating engine using the supplied named answers, and the suffix is stripped from the produced name. Files without the suffix are copied verbatim. Names of files and directories may themselves contain placeholders that are expanded into the produced path. When a plain file and a same-named file carrying the template suffix both exist, the suffixed (rendered) one takes precedence and the plain one is dropped. Output reports the count of produced files, the sorted produced paths, and the produced content of each file.

cases.expected_output:
produced=2
path: README.txt
path: greeting.txt
file: README.txt -> "Static line.\n"
file: greeting.txt -> "Hello world!\n"

produced=2
path: NOTES.txt
path: acme/__init__.py
file: NOTES.txt -> "verbatim content\n"
file: acme/__init__.py -> "name = 'acme'\n"

produced=1
path: README.txt
file: README.txt -> "Rendered for Demo.\n"

feature2_exclude_include.json.description: Selection of which template entries reach the destination. A built-in set of patterns is excluded by default (version-control metadata, compiled/cache artifacts, editor backups, the configuration file itself). An explicit exclude option drops entries matching shell-style patterns. An include option re-admits entries that the default exclusion would otherwise drop, by exact name or by pattern. The configuration file may also declare exclusion and inclusion pattern lists that drive the same selection.

cases.expected_output:
produced=2
path: .svn
path: keep.txt
file: .svn -> "svn\n"
file: keep.txt -> "keep\n"

feature3_overwrite.json.description: Behaviour when a produced file already exists in the destination. A skip-if-exists list (whose patterns are themselves rendered with the answers) preserves the pre-existing content of matching files even when overwriting is forced. A global skip mode preserves every pre-existing file, while a global force mode overwrites every pre-existing file. Output reports the final destination contents.

cases.expected_output:
produced=3
path: a.txt
path: b.txt
path: meh/c.txt
file: a.txt -> "OVERWRITTEN\n"
file: b.txt -> "SKIPPED\n"
file: meh/c.txt -> "SKIPPED\n"

feature4_pretend.json.description: A dry-run mode that performs the full rendering computation but makes no changes to the destination. Output reports that nothing was produced.

feature5_extra_paths.json.description: Resolution of template references that live outside the source tree. Additional search paths may be supplied so that a template can extend or include a shared parent template found there. If a referenced parent template cannot be located in any search path, a template-reference error is reported. If a supplied additional search path does not exist, a configuration-data error is reported categorising the offending field as not found.

feature6_config_loading.json.description: Loading of the optional configuration file from the source tree. Engine-option keys (those carrying a leading-underscore marker) and answer-default keys are both returned, each preserving its native declared type: text stays text, integers stay integers, booleans stay booleans, and sequences stay sequences. A malformed configuration file is reported as an invalid-config-file error. Two configuration files present at once are reported as a multiple-config-files error. When no configuration file is present, no keys are returned.

config_files.name: copier.yml
config_files.content:
a_string: lorem ipsum
a_number: 12345
a_boolean: true
a_list:
  - one
  - two
  - three

cases.expected_output:
keys=4
a_boolean=true
a_list=["one", "two", "three"]
a_number=12345
a_string="lorem ipsum"

feature7_flags_validation.json.description: Validation of the run-mode option set. Each mode is a strict boolean; a non-boolean value is reported as a validation error on the offending field. The overwrite-everything and preserve-everything modes are mutually exclusive and enabling both is reported as a mutual-exclusion error. Unknown extra keys are ignored. Valid input echoes the resolved mode values.

cases.expected_output:
valid=true
cleanup_on_error=true
force=true
pretend=true
quiet=false
skip=false

feature8_envops_validation.json.description: Validation of the templating-engine option set that configures the placeholder and block delimiters and toggles. Boolean toggles must be strict booleans and delimiter strings must be text; violations are reported as a validation error on the offending field. Unknown extra keys are accepted and preserved. Valid input echoes the resolved option values.

cases.expected_output:
valid=true
autoescape=true
block_end_string="%>"
block_start_string="<%"
extra_member=null
keep_trailing_newline=false
variable_end_string=">>"
variable_start_string="<<"

feature9_config_data_validation.json.description: Validation of the resolved configuration data. The source and destination locations are mandatory; omitting them is reported as missing-field errors. A source location (or extra search location) that does not exist is reported as a not-found error. Valid input echoes requested resolved fields, including the destination folder name.

_constraints.output_rule: Must produce a list of produced entries, sorted by path. Do not report paths that are excluded by default patterns.
File name suffix ".tmpl" is stripped. Content is processed by a templating engine using the `data` arguments.

_constraints.input_condition: src names contain placeholder syntax [[ name ]]
_constraints.output_rule: Placeholders within directory names and file names are expanded. The `data` map provides replacement values.
The suffixed template (rendered version) wins. The plain file is dropped from the output.
Entries matching shell-style glob patterns in the `exclude` list are dropped.
Entries matching patterns in the `include` list are re-admitted even if they match default exclusion.

_constraints.input_condition: global skip mode (skip=true) OR skip_if_exists list provided
_constraints.output_rule: Pre-existing files (via `dst_prefill`) matching the condition are NOT overwritten; their content in `expected_output` reflects original pre-existing data.
All pre-existing files (via `dst_prefill`) are overwritten with new content. `expected_output` reflects new content.
Patterns in `skip_if_exists` are rendered with `data` variables before matching.
File name is produced WITHOUT the ".tmpl" suffix (e.g., "a.txt.tmpl" becomes "a.txt").
Indicates which files already exist in the destination. Used to calculate conflicts/skips.
Preserves ALL pre-existing files. No use of `skip_if_exists` list.
Overwrites ALL pre-existing files. No use of `skip_if_exists` list.

_constraints.input_condition: src template references another file without extension (e.g., mind.txt)
_constraints.output_rule: Searches `src_path` first. If not found, searches `extra` map. If not found, throws `template_reference_not_found`.
Throws `invalid_config_data` with `field=extra_paths reason=not_found`.
Reads files, parses content. Returns keys where names have `_` prefix (engine options) or no prefix (data defaults). Preserves types (bool, int, str, list).
Throws `multiple_config_files`.

_constraints.input_condition: config content is invalid YAML/structured text
_constraints.output_rule: Throws `invalid_config_file`.

_constraints.input_condition: flag value is not boolean (e.g., string)
_constraints.output_rule: Throws `invalid_flags` with `field=X reason=value`.
Throws `invalid_flags` with `field=skip reason=mutually_exclusive` (or similar depending on which is checked last, but `skip` is explicitly named in test).
Ignored with no error.
Throws `invalid_envops` with `field=X reason=value`.
Throws `invalid_envops` with `field=X reason=type`.
Accepted and preserved in output.
Throws `invalid_config_data` with `field=X reason=missing`.

_constraints.input_condition: src_path points to non-existent real path
_constraints.output_rule: Throws `invalid_config_data` with `field=X reason=not_found`.

_constraints.input_condition: Ops are performed in order: args override config_file overrides built-in defaults.
_constraints.output_rule: Result reflects the deepest defined value.
Line: `produced=N` followed by `path: <path>`, then `file: <path> -> <json_escaped_content>`.
Paths and file outputs must be sorted lexicographically.
Returns only `produced=0`.

Must strictly follow `error=category
field=value
reason=value` (if nested) or similar structured format. No JSON or Python stack traces.
Keys must be printed in sorted order. Values rendered as JSON literal strings.
Executes the extension logic inside the block.

_constraints.default_value: Default is global exclusion patterns. _include overrides them.
